So if you've followed my work in the past, you'll know that I'm a big fan of CBO because it solves a lot of the problems around stability and scaling. The thing about CBO is it takes away some of the daily decision making and lets the machine learning within Facebook take a bit more control. ## [03:04 - 06:08]

say, you know, 46 times return on ad spend in e-commerce, is that really possible that I could put a dollar in and get $46 out or 55 or even 20? The thing is, it's as possible as your product enables it to be. He then went through and applied the strategies in the BPM method and got similar results. And this is not rocket science. This whole process of the BPM method has been written in such a simple form to enable you to go through it, whether you're running ads for yourself or for a client. This is exactly what I run. This is what my agency runs. And I'm giving that all to you on a plate, but I can only make this work for you if you follow the process. So the GTCBO method is the graduation testing campaign budget optimization method. And there's seven key parts that are involved with making this work. So going right from the top of the BPM method, we're talking about the Core 4 and the 5W avatar. Now, the Core 4 is looking at your pillars of growth. As we went through in the Core 4 training, if you are scoring below, you know, 16 out of 20 in your Core 4, you are not ready for scaling. This business that you can see with the screenshot, that business I think at an 18 out of 20 for a Core 4, we were primed for scaling and scaling is exactly what we did. But ahead of that, we also built a 5W avatar, which was clearly defined and structured. And we really, really knew who our market was, how to target them, and build that into the four funnel system. And with those three things together, we had the structure for growth. So we applied what we knew about CBO and, you know, how we use CBOs we showed in this training, and then put that through the graduation testing process. So we were researching audiences and ads and, you know, moving ads and audiences to our prospecting stage exactly as I've showed you. Then we built stability and optimization. Now, at this stage, we wanted to make sure that we had stable results on our costs, so our CPM and CPC on our conversion rates. And also we had worked on average order values. Now, for this particular store, we were doing about $120 average order value, and we couldn't increase it at the time. But that was fine, because we managed to get the cost down and the conversion rate up. And then we went into a phase which I call scale testing. Now, when you come out of stability and optimization, and we've got the whole section on that, it could be tempting to really, really push up your spend, because you know, you've got a great audience, great product, great landing page, great ads, etc., etc., that you just want to ramp up that spend. But that's where the problems occur. Now, what I do in scale testing is to bump up my budgets, and then wait, and wait for the stability, and make sure that I can sustain those results, and then bump up the budgets again, and wait, and see that I can sustain those results. And at that

## [06:08 - 08:13]

point, I start to introduce my automated rules. So if I'm getting this performance, then increase budget by this, because I've done it manually, right? I know it works. Whether I'm doing my own ecom, my own info products, or our agency clients, we are always focused on sustainable growth. And that comes through following the process.
